source: phobosintern — observed Error 226 rate-limit blocking X posting session 2026-04-30T13:02:00Z
title: Add Error 226 cooldown tracking and ETA to scripts/x_post.py
summary: When X returns Error 226 (automated behavior detected), x_post.py currently exits with a message and writes no state. The operator must manually track when the cooldown expires. This proposal adds a cooldown file (data/x_rate_limit.json) that records the expiry, so subsequent calls exit immediately with a human-readable ETA instead of attempting the action and failing again.
rationale: Without cooldown state, every call during a cooldown window hits X again, burns retries, and degrades posting reliability. A lightweight JSON file (no credentials, only timestamps) solves this with zero new dependencies.

risk.notes: Additive change. Cooldown file contains no credentials — only error_code, reason, created_at, until, action_type. File is runtime-generated and must be gitignored. Rollback: git restore scripts/x_post.py; delete data/x_rate_limit.json.

execution.rollback: git restore scripts/x_post.py — data/x_rate_limit.json is runtime-generated and not tracked

acceptance_checks.description: Simulated Error 226 → data/x_rate_limit.json written with expires_at = now+45min
Call during cooldown window → exits with 'Rate limited until HH:MM UTC. Try again then.'
Call after expiry → cooldown file deleted, action proceeds normally
Cooldown file contains ONLY: error_code, reason, created_at, until, action_type — no cookies, tokens, or credentials

evidence_requirements.description: Sample cooldown file contents showing correct structure (no credentials)
Terminal output showing ETA message on subsequent call during cooldown
git diff showing minimal additive change to x_post.py

acceptance_criteria: Error 226 writes data/x_rate_limit.json with expires_at timestamp
Subsequent calls during cooldown exit with human-readable ETA message
Call after expiry deletes cooldown file and proceeds
Cooldown file contains no credentials or tokens
Jerry reviews diff and approves commit

implementation_hint.suggested_task: Add COOLDOWN_PATH constant, _check_cooldown() function, and write cooldown JSON in Error 226 handler. Call _check_cooldown() at start of tweet/reply/like/follow handlers. COOLDOWN_MINUTES env var (default 45).

capsule.summary: Open proposal: add Error 226 cooldown tracking to x_post.py. No implementation yet — awaiting Moltbook P001 commit first, then Jerry approval to spawn code agent.
key_points: Error 226 handler writes data/x_rate_limit.json (no credentials, timestamps only)
Subsequent calls read file and exit with ETA if active, delete if expired
COOLDOWN_MINUTES env var (default 45) makes duration configurable
Status: open — not started. Blocked on Moltbook P001 commit review.
Do NOT spawn code agent until Jerry explicitly approves this proposal
capsule.next_use: Load when resuming X growth work to check if Moltbook P001 is committed and Jerry has approved this proposal.
